channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Iran's president insists Tehran does not seek a nuclear bomb and dangles US business opportunities
item.description: TEHRAN, Iran (AP) — Iran's president again pledged Wednesday that his nation is “not after a nuclear bomb” ahead of talks between Tehran and the United States, going as far as dangling the prospect of direct American investment in the Islamic Republic if the countries can reach a deal.
item.dc:creator: NASSER KARIMI and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Beijing rejects Ukraine's claim significant numbers of Chinese troops are fighting alongside Russia
item.description: Ukraine’s claim that significant numbers of Chinese nationals are fighting alongside Russia’s invading army is “totally unfounded,” a Beijing official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: More than 900 illegal aliens charged with immigration-related crimes during first week of April: DOJ
item.description: US attorneys in six districts filed immigration-related charges against nearly 1,000 illegal aliens during the first week of April under Operation Take Back America, the DOJ said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Salt Lake Bees' new stadium in Daybreak expected to bring economic impacts, growth to local businesses
item.description: South Jordan is home to local businesses and neighbors who say they're starting to feel the effects of increased foot traffic from a new baseball stadium.
item.dc:creator: McKenzie Diaz, ABC 4

item.title: Delphi exhibits include Richard Allen's confession tapes, police interviews
item.description: Allen would go on to make these confessions in several more of the released prison calls. These include calls made on June 5 and June 11, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Matt Adams, NewsNation

item.title: Surgery nightmare: Hospital admits mistake after failed cancer surgery attempt
item.description: An investigation is underway at MountainView Hospital in Las Vegas after a cancer patient woke up to startling news in the operating room.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Murphy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Microsoft fires employees who protested its support for Israel at 50th anniversary event
item.description: Two Microsoft employees were fired for protests condemning the company’s support for Israel at a 50th anniversary event last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KREX 5

item.title: Judge tells government to provide evidence, or case against Columbia student Mahmoud Khalil is over
item.description: JENA, La. (AP) — An immigration judge in Louisiana said she would terminate the case against Mahmoud Khalil if the government does not provide evidence this week justifying their attempted deportation of the Columbia University student activist.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Keystone oil pipeline shutdown could quickly lead to higher gasoline prices
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — The nearly 2,700-mile Keystone oil pipeline was shut down Tuesday morning after it ruptured in North Dakota, halting the flow of millions of gallons of crude oil from Canada to refineries in the U.S. and potentially leading to higher gasoline prices.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and SARAH RAZA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Human remains found in 1973 identified as missing Kansas teen
item.description: Human remains found near Garnett, Kansas, in 1973 have been positively identified more than 50 years later.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TSA Officers find hidden weapons inside cane
item.description: TSA officers at the Shreveport Regional Airport security checkpoint discovered a cache of weapons, including a gutting knife, a double-edged blade, and a knife that could function as a small machete, all hidden inside a metal cane.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Cheng, NewsNation

item.title: Agreement in works to settle Northwestern hazing lawsuits that led to football coach's firing
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former Northwestern University football players are finalizing an agreement with the school to settle lawsuits alleging hazing and abuse that led to longtime coach Pat Fitzgerald’s firing, a school spokesman and attorneys said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man arrested for allegedly distributing fentanyl, meth near Jordan River Trail
item.description: A man was arrested on Monday for allegedly distributing drugs — including suspected fentanyl and methamphetamine — near the Jordan River Trail.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: New Mexico governor mobilizes National Guard to tackle crime emergency in Albuquerque
item.description: Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham has declared a state of emergency in New Mexico's largest city, saying that a significant increase in crime in Albuquerque warrants the help of the New Mexico National Guard.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump touts Supreme Court deportation ruling as a major victory, but legal fight is far from over
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is touting a Supreme Court ruling allowing it to resume deportations under the Alien Enemies Act as a major victory, but the immigration fight is far from over.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Parents arrested, charged after third grader brings gun to a elementary school
item.description: Both parents were charged with recklessly leaving a loaded, unsecured firearm in a manner that endangered the life or safety of a child under 14.
item.dc:creator: Kendal McAuley, NewsNation

item.title: Keystone pipeline shut down after North Dakota rupture
item.description: The affected segment has been isolated, and operations and containment resources have been mobilized to site,” the company said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-member of former Gambia dictator's military unit is on trial in the US for torture
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A man accused of torturing people suspected in a planned coup against Gambia’s longtime leader was a low-ranking private in the West African country’s military who risked torture and death himself if he disobeyed superiors, a lawyer for the defendant told jurors Tuesday in opening stat
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House insists Trump won't budge on 104 percent China tariff
item.description: The White House on Tuesday insisted that President Trump won’t back down on his threat to impose a more than 100 percent tariff rate on China, significantly escalating the trade war between the two nations hours before the deadline approaches.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: National Weather Service no longer translating products for non-English speakers
item.description: The National Weather Service is no longer providing language translations of its products, a change that experts say could put non-English speakers at risk of missing potentially life-saving warnings about extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Last chance to claim $1,400 stimulus check from 2021 nears
item.description: Taxpayers who haven’t filed their 2021 tax returns may still be eligible to receive one (or more) stimulus payments sent during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KRON4

item.title: Supreme Court clears way for Trump admin to fire some probationary workers
item.description: The Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration Tuesday by enabling officials to fire thousands of federal workers in their probationary period, saying the government employee unions that sued don’t have legal standing.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Photos: 'Jackson Magnolia' removed at White House, replaced with descendant sapling
item.description: The newest greenery added to the White House grounds came the day after one of the southern magnolia trees, which was said to have been planted by Jackson in the 19th century, was removed due to "serious safety concerns.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US expected a big travel year, but overseas visitors
item.description: Olja Ivanic looked forward to welcoming some cousins from Sweden to her Denver home in June. Ivanic and the four travelers were planning to go hiking in Colorado and then visit Los Angeles and San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Future uncertain for Duke basketball star due to new Trump policy
item.description: A new visa policy announced by the Trump administration last weekend could impact South Sudanese passport holders, including Duke basketball phenom Khaman Maluach.
item.dc:creator: Keaton Eberly, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Boris Johnson pecked by ostrich
item.description: Former British Prime Minister Boris Johnson had a fowl encounter with an ostrich during a sight-seeing trip in Texas, with video showing him getting nipped by the bird while his son laughed in his lap.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ICE agent impersonations spike in wake of immigration crackdown
item.description: Impersonatorsposing as immigration agents are spreading fear among migrants already uncertain about their future in the country.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans rattled by tariff troubles begin to push back on Trump
item.description: Cracks are growing between congressional Republicans and President Trump over his tariff policy, with some lawmakers looking to strip him of his unilateral tariff authority and others seeking briefings from the White House on the strategy amid growing economic tumult.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Clem Burke, multifaceted drummer of iconic rock group Blondie, has died
item.description: Clem Burke, whose versatile drumming propelled the iconic rock group Blondie during its decades performing everything from new-wave punk to disco-infused tunes, has died. He was 70. The band said in a statement on its website Monday that he died from cancer but no additional details were provided.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man, 71, arrested after 7 'emotional support' tigers found in his house
item.description: A 71-year-old man from Nevada was arrested last week after authorities seized seven tigers, which he claimed were all “emotional support animals,” that he kept in his home, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' Underground Railroad web page
item.description: The National Parks Service (NPS) has removed a reference to abolitionist Harriet Tubman from its webpage dedicated to the Underground Railroad.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KTLA 5

item.title: Chief Justice Roberts pauses deadline for return of Maryland man mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chief Justice John Roberts agreed Monday to pause a midnight deadline for the Trump administration to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Epic Universe opens a portal to the future of theme parks
item.description: As Universal Orlando Resort gears up for the highly anticipated May 22 opening of Epic Universe, it’s clear the resort is entering a new era - one that could redefine what a theme park can offer families.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, Fox 5

item.title: Jay North, TV's mischievous Dennis the Menace, dies at 73
item.description: LAKE BUTLER, Fla. (AP) — Jay North, who starred as the towheaded mischief maker on TV's “Dennis the Menace” for four seasons starting in 1959, has died. He was 73.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Chief Justice Roberts pauses order to return Maryland dad mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: Chief Justice John Roberts agreed Monday to pause a midnight deadline for the Trump administration to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: 6-year-old found wandering alone with dog in Florida; mom, 1 other charged
item.description: The Volusia Sheriff's Office said a 6-year-old girl and her dog were found wandering alone on Friday afternoon in Deltona.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, ABC 4
